crisis
war
peace_treaty
economic_crash
civilization_shift






# FILE: 030.model/020.catalog/MODEL_ID_RULE.md

# ============================================================
# MODEL ID STRUCTURE
# ============================================================

Model ID format


SSDDDMMMM


SS   system
DDD  domain
MMMM model


Example

0150000001


system      = 01
layer       = 50
domain      = 000
model       = 0001


Benefits

Stable IDs
Cross-system reference
Future extension






# FILE: 030.model/020.catalog/MODEL_LAYER_OVERVIEW.md

# ============================================================
# CIVILIZATION MODEL LAYER OVERVIEW
# ============================================================

status: canonical
system_id: 01
layer: model
scope: civilization.model.layer

owner: Boss
prepared_by: Zero


# PURPOSE

Define the structure of the CivilizationOS model layer.

The model layer represents all entities that exist
within CivilizationOS.


# MODEL STRUCTURE

Model Layer is organized by domains.

000 - 120


# DOMAIN STRUCTURE

Each domain contains a MODEL BOOK.

MODEL BOOK defines

models
states
events
relations
metrics


# MODEL COUNT RULE

000-111

25 models per domain


112-120

50 models per domain


# MODEL TOTAL

Approximate total models

3250+


# DESIGN PRINCIPLES

Explicit structure

Stable identifiers

Domain isolation

Deterministic relations

Fail-safe model evolution






# FILE: 030.model/020.catalog/MODEL_RELATION_OVERVIEW.md

# ============================================================
# MODEL RELATION OVERVIEW
# ============================================================

Models interact through defined relations.

Types


ownership

membership

dependency

flow

governance


Example


person -> household

household -> housing

person -> labor

labor -> industry

industry -> economy

economy -> nation


Cross domain relations must remain explicit.

Implicit relations are forbidden.






# FILE: 030.model/020.catalog/MODEL_STATE_CATALOG.md

# ============================================================

layer: model

owner: Boss
prepared_by: Zero

# HUMAN STATES

birth
childhood
education
employment
career_growth
retirement
deceased


# ORGANIZATION STATES

created
active
suspended
restructured
dissolved


# ECONOMIC STATES

stable
growth
recession
crisis
collapse


# TECHNOLOGY STATES

research
development
testing
deployment
deprecated


# CIVILIZATION STATES

formation
expansion
stability
decline
transformation






# FILE: 030.model/030.binding/0150000036_CIVILIZATION_MODEL_EXECUTION_BINDING.md

# ============================================================
# CIVILIZATION MODEL EXECUTION BINDING
# ============================================================

status: canonical
system_id: 01
domain_id: 5000
model_id: 0036
scope: civilization.model.execution-binding
owner: Boss
prepared_by: Zero


# PURPOSE

Define how models bind to executable runtime behavior.


# RULE

models do not execute directly

models bind to:

entity instances
state machines
event handlers
relation propagation
metric derivation


# RESULT

The model layer remains conceptual,
but fully executable through runtime binding.





# FILE: 030.model/030.binding/0150000037_CIVILIZATION_MODEL_STATE_BINDING.md

# ============================================================
# CIVILIZATION MODEL STATE BINDING
# ============================================================

status: canonical
system_id: 01
domain_id: 5000
model_id: 0037
scope: civilization.model.state-binding
owner: Boss
prepared_by: Zero


# PURPOSE

Define how models map to states.


# RULE

each executable model may bind to:

state set
transition rules
terminal states
recovery states
crisis states


# EXAMPLES

company
stable
growth
crisis
collapsed

nation
formation
stability
crisis
collapse





# FILE: 030.model/030.binding/0150000038_CIVILIZATION_MODEL_EVENT_BINDING.md

# ============================================================
# CIVILIZATION MODEL EVENT BINDING
# ============================================================

status: canonical
system_id: 01
domain_id: 5000
model_id: 0038
scope: civilization.model.event-binding
owner: Boss
prepared_by: Zero


# PURPOSE

Define how models bind to event types.


# RULE

each major model class must declare
compatible event families.

examples:

company
market_entry
bankruptcy
merger

bank
bank_run
liquidity_crisis
resolution

nation
election
conflict
reform





# FILE: 030.model/030.binding/0150000039_CIVILIZATION_MODEL_METRIC_BINDING.md

# ============================================================
# CIVILIZATION MODEL METRIC BINDING
# ============================================================

status: canonical
system_id: 01
domain_id: 5000
model_id: 0039
scope: civilization.model.metric-binding
owner: Boss
prepared_by: Zero


# PURPOSE

Define how models contribute to metrics.


# RULE

metrics are derived from:

entity states
relations
events
policy effects

models specify eligible metric families,
not direct metric values.





# FILE: 030.model/030.binding/0150000040_CIVILIZATION_MODEL_POLICY_BINDING.md

# ============================================================
# CIVILIZATION MODEL POLICY BINDING
# ============================================================

status: canonical
system_id: 01
domain_id: 5000
model_id: 0040
scope: civilization.model.policy-binding
owner: Boss
prepared_by: Zero


# PURPOSE

Define how models are affected by policy.


# RULE

policy modifies:

constraints
transition bias
resource allocation
event probability
interaction rules

policy must not directly mutate canonical model semantics.





# FILE: 030.model/030.binding/0150000110_CIVILIZATION_CANONICAL_MODEL_BINDING.md

# CIVILIZATION CANONICAL MODEL BINDING

status: canonical
layer: model-binding
domain: global

## PURPOSE
Bind high-volume model catalog and canonical model layer.

## RULE
- 050.model is the broad catalog layer
- 030.model is the canonical operational layer
- runtime and interface must reference canonical models first




# FILE: 030.model/030.binding/0150000120_CIVILIZATION_MODEL_TO_RUNTIME_BINDING.md

# CIVILIZATION MODEL TO RUNTIME BINDING

status: canonical
layer: model-binding
domain: runtime

## RULES
- runtime reads canonical IDs
- runtime state transitions must map to canonical models
- execution result must reference stable model ownership




# FILE: 030.model/030.binding/0150000130_CIVILIZATION_MODEL_TO_POLICY_BINDING.md

# CIVILIZATION MODEL TO POLICY BINDING

status: canonical
layer: model-binding
domain: policy

## RULES
- policy never mutates anonymous structures
- policy targets must map to explicit canonical models
- enforcement must be auditable




# FILE: 030.model/030.binding/0150000140_CIVILIZATION_MODEL_TO_INTERFACE_BINDING.md

# CIVILIZATION MODEL TO INTERFACE BINDING

status: canonical
layer: model-binding
domain: interface

## RULES
- interface contracts must map to canonical models
- view names may vary, underlying model reference must not
- interface adapters must remain explicit

# FILE: 030.runtime/060.integration/061_CIVILIZATION_SYNC_CONSUMER_RUNTIME.md

# CIVILIZATION SYNC CONSUMER RUNTIME

status: canonical
layer: runtime
domain: integration
owner: Boss
prepared_by: Zero

## RUNTIME STEPS
1. receive Persona sync event
2. validate event contract
3. update mirrored Persona reference records
4. recalculate listing, purchase, and access availability
5. record consume result




# FILE: 040.flow/040.persona/041_PERSONA_SNAPSHOT_PRODUCT_REGISTRATION_FLOW.md

# PERSONA SNAPSHOT PRODUCT REGISTRATION FLOW

status: canonical
layer: flow
domain: persona
owner: Boss
prepared_by: Zero

## FLOW
1. PersonaOS marks a releasable snapshot as externally usable
2. CivilizationOS syncs release reference
3. product definition is created using existing PRODUCT model
4. Persona license boundary is checked
5. listing becomes available only when release and license states are valid




# FILE: 040.flow/040.persona/042_PERSONA_SNAPSHOT_PURCHASE_FLOW.md

# PERSONA SNAPSHOT PURCHASE FLOW

status: canonical
layer: flow
domain: persona
owner: Boss
prepared_by: Zero

## FLOW
1. buyer selects Persona-derived product
2. CivilizationOS validates release ref and license ref state
3. purchase and transaction are created using existing commerce models
4. payment completes
5. access grant sync is requested
6. usage becomes available only after PersonaOS-valid access state is confirmed




# FILE: 040.flow/040.persona/043_PERSONA_SNAPSHOT_ACCESS_SYNC_FLOW.md

# PERSONA SNAPSHOT ACCESS SYNC FLOW

status: canonical
layer: flow
domain: persona
owner: Boss
prepared_by: Zero

## FLOW
1. PersonaOS issues or updates access grant state
2. CivilizationOS receives sync event or polling result
3. PERSONA_ACCESS_GRANT_REF_MODEL is updated
4. product usage availability is recalculated
5. expired or revoked access immediately disables use




# FILE: 040.runtime/000.core/000_RUNTIME_OVERVIEW.md

status: canonical
layer: runtime

Defines the canonical runtime layer of CivilizationOS.

Principles:
- event-driven
- registry authoritative
- no direct hidden mutation
- state changes through controlled execution




# FILE: 040.runtime/000.core/010_RUNTIME_PRINCIPLES.md

status: canonical
layer: runtime

- default time = real-time
- event priority must be deterministic
- runtime consumes model + policy + integration




# FILE: 040.runtime/010.event/010_EVENT_REGISTRY.md

status: canonical
layer: runtime

Defines authoritative event types and payload contracts.




# FILE: 040.runtime/010.event/020_EVENT_PIPELINE.md

status: canonical
layer: runtime

Flow:
trigger -> event -> queue -> dispatcher -> handler -> state update




# FILE: 040.runtime/010.event/030_EVENT_PRIORITY.md

status: canonical
layer: runtime

Priority:
- death
- illness
- job
- education
- other




# FILE: 040.runtime/020.time/010_TIME_RUNTIME.md

status: canonical
layer: runtime

- default mode = real-time
- configurable scale
- time tick drives scheduled events




# FILE: 040.runtime/030.execution/010_EXECUTION_ENGINE.md

status: canonical
layer: runtime

Execution is rule-based and deterministic.




# FILE: 040.runtime/030.execution/020_DISPATCH_RUNTIME.md

status: canonical
layer: runtime

Dispatcher resolves handler by event type.




# FILE: 040.runtime/030_RUNTIME_INDEX.md

# RUNTIME INDEX

status: canonical
layer: index
owner: Boss
prepared_by: Zero

status: canonical
layer: runtime

# FILE: 040.runtime/060.audit/010_RUNTIME_AUDIT.md

status: canonical
layer: runtime

Critical runtime actions must be auditable.




# FILE: 040.runtime/070.integration/010_EXTERNAL_RUNTIME_LINK.md

layer: runtime

# FILE: 040.runtime/080.control/010_RUNTIME_CONTROL.md

status: canonical
layer: runtime

Administrative runtime controls must not bypass canonical event flow.




# FILE: 040.runtime/090.observability/010_RUNTIME_OBSERVABILITY.md

layer: runtime

Runtime exposes logs, metrics, queue state, and failure signals.

status: canonical
layer: flow

# FILE: 050.flow/010.persona/010_PERSONA_LIFECYCLE_FLOW.md

status: canonical
layer: flow

persona reference -> stat interpretation -> events -> snapshot / reincarnation rules




# FILE: 050.flow/010.world/010_NATION_FLOW.md

# ============================================================
# NATION FLOW
# ============================================================

User → Nation Creation Request → Validation → Nation Created





# FILE: 050.flow/020.education/010_EDUCATION_FLOW.md

status: canonical
layer: flow

ticket -> exam -> result -> eligibility / certification




# FILE: 050.flow/030.career/010_CAREER_FLOW.md

status: canonical
layer: flow

application -> interview -> evaluation -> employment -> income




# FILE: 050.flow/040.economy/010_ECONOMY_FLOW.md

# ============================================================
# ECONOMY FLOW
# ============================================================

Product → Purchase → Payment → Revenue Ledger

Rules:

- no mixed payment
- one currency per product
- revenue separated by currency type





# FILE: 050.flow/040.economy/010_PURCHASE_FLOW.md

status: canonical
layer: flow

product -> purchase -> transaction -> ledger -> revenue




# FILE: 050.flow/040.economy/020_STOCK_FLOW.md

status: canonical
layer: flow

stock buy -> holding update -> dividend distribution




# FILE: 050.flow/040.economy/030_REVENUE_SETTLEMENT_FLOW.md

# REVENUE SETTLEMENT FLOW

status: canonical
layer: flow
domain: economy

## FLOW
1. revenue is recorded
2. company ledger is updated
3. settlement batch is generated
4. monthly closing is executed
5. receivable becomes payable
6. payout instruction is produced
7. settlement result is audited




# FILE: 050.flow/040.economy/040_TAX_COLLECTION_FLOW.md

# TAX COLLECTION FLOW

status: canonical
layer: flow
domain: economy

## FLOW
1. taxable event occurs
2. tax base is calculated
3. tax rule is resolved
4. tax amount is posted
5. treasury ledger is updated
6. audit event is recorded




# FILE: 050.flow/040.economy/050_BANKRUPTCY_FLOW.md

# BANKRUPTCY FLOW

status: canonical
layer: flow
domain: economy

## FLOW
1. insolvency condition is detected
2. review rule is applied
3. bankruptcy state is proposed
4. approval or automatic threshold rule is applied
5. company state changes
6. market and ledger impact is recorded

# FILE: 050.flow/060.government/020_ELECTION_FLOW.md

# ELECTION FLOW

status: canonical
layer: flow
domain: government

## FLOW
1. election schedule is opened
2. candidate eligibility is verified
3. campaign phase begins
4. voting window opens
5. votes are counted
6. result is certified
7. authority state is updated




# FILE: 050.flow/060.government/030_BUDGET_ALLOCATION_FLOW.md

# BUDGET ALLOCATION FLOW

status: canonical
layer: flow
domain: government

## FLOW
1. treasury status is confirmed
2. budget proposal is created
3. allocation review occurs
4. approval is finalized
5. ledger distribution is posted
6. downstream execution begins




# FILE: 050.flow/060.government/040_TREATY_FLOW.md

# TREATY FLOW

status: canonical
layer: flow
domain: government

## FLOW
1. treaty draft is proposed
2. members are identified
3. obligations are negotiated
4. treaty is ratified
5. effect becomes active
6. violation monitoring begins




# FILE: 050.flow/070.runtime/010_EVENT_EXECUTION_FLOW.md

status: canonical
layer: flow

event queue -> execution -> state update -> audit / notification




# FILE: 050.flow/080.law/010_CRIME_TO_PENALTY_FLOW.md

# CRIME TO PENALTY FLOW

status: canonical
layer: flow
domain: law

## FLOW
1. incident is reported
2. evidence is collected
3. violation class is determined
4. law rule is evaluated
5. penalty is assigned
6. enforcement action is recorded




# FILE: 050.flow/080.law/020_CONTENT_REGULATION_FLOW.md

# CONTENT REGULATION FLOW

status: canonical
layer: flow
domain: law

## FLOW
1. content is submitted or detected
2. rule engine evaluates content
3. moderation state is assigned
4. action is taken
5. appeal path remains available
6. audit record is written




# FILE: 050.flow/085.war/010_WAR_DECLARATION_FLOW.md

# WAR DECLARATION FLOW

status: canonical
layer: flow
domain: war

## FLOW
1. hostile action threshold is reached
2. declaration proposal is prepared
3. authority rule is checked
4. declaration is issued
5. war state becomes active
6. world status is updated




# FILE: 050.flow/085.war/020_BATTLE_RESOLUTION_FLOW.md

# BATTLE RESOLUTION FLOW

status: canonical
layer: flow
domain: war

## FLOW
1. battle encounter is created
2. participating forces are fixed
3. modifiers are applied
4. resolution logic executes
5. casualty and territory changes are recorded
6. war ledger is updated




# FILE: 050.flow/090.security/010_ACCESS_VIOLATION_RESPONSE_FLOW.md

# ACCESS VIOLATION RESPONSE FLOW

status: canonical
layer: flow
domain: security

## FLOW
1. violation signal is detected
2. actor and target are identified
3. severity is classified
4. access is blocked or constrained
5. incident record is created
6. review is triggered




# FILE: 050.flow/095.meta/010_SEASON_RESET_FLOW.md

# SEASON RESET FLOW

status: canonical
layer: flow
domain: meta

## FLOW
1. season close checkpoint is reached
2. ranking is finalized
3. rewards are calculated
4. reset targets are determined
5. next season state is initialized




# FILE: 050.flow/095.meta/020_RANKING_REWARD_FLOW.md

# RANKING REWARD FLOW

status: canonical
layer: flow
domain: meta

## FLOW
1. ranking snapshot is taken
2. reward tier is resolved
3. entitlement is generated
4. reward is distributed
5. distribution log is audited




# FILE: 050.model/040.persona/PERSONA_ACCESS_GRANT_REF_MODEL.md

# PERSONA ACCESS GRANT REF MODEL

status: canonical
layer: model
domain: persona
owner: Boss
prepared_by: Zero

## PURPOSE
Reference external access grant state for Persona-derived usage.

## FIELDS
- persona_access_grant_ref_id
- persona_release_ref_id
- holder_type
- holder_id
- access_scope
- access_state
- granted_at
- expires_at
- synced_at

## RULE
Access state must be synchronized from PersonaOS.
CivilizationOS must deny use when grant is inactive or expired.




# FILE: 050.model/040.persona/PERSONA_LICENSE_REF_MODEL.md

# PERSONA LICENSE REF MODEL

status: canonical
layer: model
domain: persona
owner: Boss
prepared_by: Zero

## PURPOSE
Reference PersonaOS license state needed by CivilizationOS.

## FIELDS
- persona_license_ref_id
- persona_release_ref_id
- license_code
- license_type
- entitlement_state
- transfer_allowed
- revoked
- effective_from
- effective_to
- synced_at

## RULE
CivilizationOS reads this model for commercial enforcement.
PersonaOS remains the source of truth.




# FILE: 050.model/040.persona/PERSONA_RELEASE_REF_MODEL.md

# PERSONA RELEASE REF MODEL

status: canonical
layer: model
domain: persona
owner: Boss
prepared_by: Zero

## PURPOSE
Reference a releasable Persona artifact managed by PersonaOS.

## FIELDS
- persona_release_ref_id
- persona_id
- snapshot_id
- release_unit_type
- external_release_code
- signature_state
- release_state
- usable_from
- usable_to
- synced_at

## RULE
This model is a reference mirror only.
Original release authority remains in PersonaOS.




# FILE: 050.model/060.integration/CIVILIZATION_SYNC_EVENT_INBOX_MODEL.md

# CIVILIZATION_SYNC_EVENT_INBOX_MODEL

status: canonical
layer: model
domain: integration
owner: Boss
prepared_by: Zero

## PURPOSE
Represent inbound sync events consumed by CivilizationOS.

## FIELDS
- civilization_sync_event_inbox_id
- source_system
- event_type
- aggregate_type
- aggregate_id
- payload_json
- consume_state
- attempt_count
- next_retry_at
- received_at
- consumed_at




# FILE: 060.integration/000.core/000_INTEGRATION_OVERVIEW.md

status: canonical

All systems integrate via:

- DB (state)
- Event (behavior)
- Policy (rules)

No direct coupling allowed.




# FILE: 060.integration/000.core/010_DEPENDENCY_FLOW.md

# ============================================================
# DEPENDENCY FLOW
# ============================================================

PersonaSystem → Civilization → Applications

Internal flow:

world → economy → player → runtime

Forbidden:

- reverse dependency
- direct mutation across domains





# FILE: 060.integration/000.core/020_CROSS_DOMAIN_RULES.md

# ============================================================
# CROSS DOMAIN RULES
# ============================================================

- persona affects:
  → career
  → relationship
  → expense

- nation affects:
  → law
  → economy
  → censorship

- economy affects:
  → lifestyle
  → company
  → growth opportunity





# FILE: 060.integration/000.core/090_INTEGRATION_SUMMARY.md

# ============================================================
# INTEGRATION SUMMARY
# ============================================================

System is fully connected via:

- persona reference
- event-driven runtime
- DB-controlled values

No domain owns another domain improperly.





# FILE: 060.integration/000_INTEGRATION_OVERVIEW.md

# INTEGRATION OVERVIEW

status: canonical
layer: integration
owner: Boss
prepared_by: Zero

## PURPOSE
Define the integration responsibility of Civilization OS.

## RULE
Integration is responsible only for:
- domain-to-domain connection
- adapter contracts
- boundary mapping
- dependency direction
- payload translation

## NOT IN SCOPE
- business flow
- runtime lifecycle
- user interaction sequence
- policy text itself

## DEPENDENCY RULE
flow -> integration is allowed for reference
integration -> flow is not canonical ownership

status: canonical
layer: integration
domain: world

## CONNECTS
- foundation
- government
- economy
- war
- meta

## RULES
- world entities must use canonical IDs
- world state changes must be explicit and auditable

# FILE: 060.operations/040.persona/041_PERSONA_LICENSE_SYNC_OPERATION.md

# PERSONA LICENSE SYNC OPERATION

status: canonical
layer: operations
domain: persona
owner: Boss
prepared_by: Zero

## PURPOSE
Synchronize PersonaOS license state into CivilizationOS reference models.

## PROCEDURE
- identify changed license records from PersonaOS
- update PERSONA_LICENSE_REF_MODEL
- verify effective period and revocation state
- record sync timestamp
- notify dependent product/purchase enforcement if state changed




# FILE: 060.operations/040.persona/042_PERSONA_RELEASE_PUBLISH_OPERATION.md

# PERSONA RELEASE PUBLISH OPERATION

status: canonical
layer: operations
domain: persona
owner: Boss
prepared_by: Zero

## PURPOSE
Publish Persona-derived release references for CivilizationOS commerce.

## PROCEDURE
- confirm release eligibility from PersonaOS
- sync release reference
- verify signature and usable state
- register product mapping
- enable listing only after validation success




# FILE: 060.operations/060.integration/061_CIVILIZATION_SYNC_CONSUME_OPERATION.md

# CIVILIZATION SYNC CONSUME OPERATION

status: canonical
layer: operations
domain: integration
owner: Boss
prepared_by: Zero

## PROCEDURE
- read pending inbox events
- validate Persona sync payload
- update mirrored release, license, and access grant refs
- recalculate dependent product and usage availability
- record consume result

# FILE: 070.operations/080.security/010_KEY_ROTATION_OPERATIONS.md

# KEY ROTATION OPERATIONS

status: canonical
layer: operations
domain: security

## PURPOSE
Define operational rotation of signing and encryption keys.

- prepare new key
- validate compatibility
- rotate active key
- re-sign if necessary
- revoke old key
- write audit trail

- audit
- review postmortem

domain: data

- run scheduled backup
- verify integrity
- store recovery metadata
- execute restore drill
- document results




# FILE: 070.operations/090.data/020_RETENTION_AND_PURGE_OPERATIONS.md

# RETENTION AND PURGE OPERATIONS

status: canonical
layer: operations
domain: data

- retention period must be explicit
- purge must be auditable
- canonical entities prefer logical delete
- security and audit logs follow policy retention

# RELEASE OPERATIONS

status: canonical
layer: operations
domain: release

- execute validation
- approve release
- deploy
- monitor
- rollback if needed

# MIGRATION OPERATIONS

status: canonical
layer: operations
domain: release

- run migration
- validate post-state
- document version uplift

# FILE: 070.policy/040.persona/041_PERSONA_EXTERNAL_USAGE_POLICY.md

# PERSONA EXTERNAL USAGE POLICY

status: canonical
layer: policy
domain: persona
owner: Boss
prepared_by: Zero

## PURPOSE
Define policy for external commercial use of Persona-derived artifacts.

## RULES
- Persona original state remains owned by PersonaOS
- only releasable external units may be sold or exposed
- CivilizationOS may commercialize usage but may not override Persona rights
- invalid, revoked, or expired Persona access must disable use
- external use must respect PersonaOS license and access grant state




# FILE: 070.policy/060.integration/061_CIVILIZATION_SYNC_CONSUMER_POLICY.md

# CIVILIZATION SYNC CONSUMER POLICY

status: canonical
layer: policy
domain: integration
owner: Boss
prepared_by: Zero

## RULES
- Persona mirrored state must be updated only from approved sync events
- invalid or unverifiable payload must not mutate local truth
- failed sync consumption must remain observable and retryable
- revoked Persona rights state must disable Civilization-side usage

# CIVILIZATION POLICY CANONICAL

# ./070_POLICY_OVERVIEW_CANONICAL.md

# ============================================================
# CIVILIZATION POLICY OVERVIEW
# ============================================================

status: canonical
scope: civilization.policy

owner: Boss
prepared_by: Zero


# PURPOSE

Define governance rules of Civilization OS.


# POLICY ROLE

Policy layer defines system control rules.

Architecture defines structure.
Runtime executes behavior.
Policy defines permission and restriction.


# POLICY DOMAINS

Authority Policy  
Access Policy  
Security Policy  
Audit Policy  
Failure Policy  
Evolution Policy


# DESIGN PRINCIPLE

Fail Closed  
Explicit Control  
Traceable Governance  
Deterministic Behavior



---

# ./071_AUTHORITY_POLICY.md

# ============================================================
# AUTHORITY POLICY
# ============================================================

status: canonical
version: 1.0
compatibility: STRICT
scope: civilization.policy.authority
component: authority-policy
document_id: CIV-POL-071
owner: Boss
prepared_by: Zero
last_updated: 2026-03-10


# COMPATIBILITY

Must comply with authority model defined in
the Constitution layer.


# ABSTRACT

Defines policy governing authority delegation,
assignment, and use within CivilizationOS.


# STRUCTURE

Authority classes include:

human authority
system authority
ai advisory authority

Authority scope includes:

decision authority
operational authority
approval authority


# IMPLEMENTATION

Authority assignments are defined through
identity and governance bindings.


# CONSTRAINTS

Authority must remain explicit and auditable.

# ./080_EVENT_DEDUPLICATION_POLICY.md

# ============================================================
# EVENT DEDUPLICATION POLICY
# ============================================================

status: canonical
scope: civilization.policy.event_deduplication
component: event-deduplication-policy
owner: Boss
prepared_by: Zero


# PURPOSE

Ensures that duplicate events do not produce repeated state mutation.


# DUPLICATE SOURCES

Duplicate events may occur due to

network retry  
dispatcher retry  
worker restart  
message re-delivery


# IDENTIFICATION

Events are uniquely identified by

event_id

Optionally verified using

event_hash


# POLICY RULES

If duplicate event detected

reject execution


Duplicate detection may occur at

ingestion layer  
dispatcher layer  
execution layer


# SAFETY PRINCIPLE

Event execution must be idempotent.


# END
# ============================================================


---

# ./081_CIVILIZATION_MACRO_GOVERNOR_POLICY.md

# ============================================================
# CIVILIZATION MACRO GOVERNOR POLICY
# Triple Civilization Balancer
# ============================================================

status: canonical
version: 1.0
compatibility: STRICT
scope: civilization.policy.macro_governor
component: civilization-macro-governor
document_id: CIV-POL-081
owner: Boss
prepared_by: Zero
last_updated: 2026-03-11


# ============================================================
# ABSTRACT
# ============================================================

Defines the Triple Macro Governor system.

The Triple is a macro balancing mechanism
that stabilizes civilization dynamics.

The governor regulates:

Production
Economy
Reproduction

The system never acts as a ruler.

Its role is to maintain long-term stability
of civilization.


# ============================================================
# CORE CONCEPT
# ============================================================

The Triple represents the macro stabilizer
of the CivilizationOS ecosystem.

It monitors global system metrics and applies
indirect corrections when systemic imbalance occurs.


# ============================================================
# THREE AXES OF CONTROL
# ============================================================

Axis 1

Production Control

Axis 2

Economic Control

Axis 3

Reproduction Control


# ============================================================
# PRODUCTION CONTROL
# ============================================================

Targets

• Production volume  
• Resource consumption  
• Labor allocation  
• Inventory saturation  

Objective

Prevent resource collapse and
runaway overproduction.

Control methods

• world-level event throughput control  
• production event priority adjustment  
• growth-axis indirect correction  

Restriction

Direct inventory modification is forbidden.


# ============================================================
# ECONOMIC CONTROL
# ============================================================

Targets

• Credit expansion  
• nation_credit spikes  
• wealth concentration  
• transaction overload  

Objective

Prevent economic bubbles and credit collapse.

Control methods

• nation_credit adjustment coefficient  
• decay intensity adjustment  
• migration cooldown tuning  
• transaction event weight control  

Restriction

Direct currency issuance is forbidden.


# ============================================================
# REPRODUCTION CONTROL
# ============================================================

Targets

• Persona growth rate  
• AI generation frequency  
• Snapshot issuance rate  
• Civilization onboarding speed  

Objective

Prevent uncontrolled population expansion
and identity inflation.

Control methods

• Snapshot issuance limits  
• AI generation throttling  
• growth rate modifiers  

Restriction

Persona deletion is forbidden.


# ============================================================
# DUAL MODE OPERATION
# ============================================================

Mode A

CEO Mode (Normal Operation)

• Focus on business world  
• Monitor KPIs  
• Operate approval hubs  
• Observe economic indicators  

Mode B

Civilization Mode (Emergency)

• Growth regulation  
• Decay amplification  
• Migration throttling  
• World priority adjustment  


# ============================================================
# MODE SWITCH CONDITIONS
# ============================================================

Civilization mode activates when:

average stability < threshold

trust deviation > threshold

nation_credit variance > threshold

event throughput spike detected


# ============================================================
# SYSTEM IMPLEMENTATION
# ============================================================

Core modules

civilization.macro_control

civilization.system_policy

civilization.health_index

civilization.emergency_flag


Edge Function

civilization-macro-governor


Database Tables

civilization.global_metrics

civilization.adjustment_log


# ============================================================
# CIVILIZATION HEALTH INDEX
# ============================================================

Civilization Health Index (CHI)

CHI =

  0.4 * average_stability
+ 0.3 * inverse_trust_variance
+ 0.2 * nation_credit_stability
+ 0.1 * event_load_stability


CHI < 0.4

→ Emergency Mode


# ============================================================
# PHILOSOPHY
# ============================================================

The Triple is not a ruler.

It is a stabilizer.

Its role is to protect civilization
while preserving individual personas.


# ============================================================
# END OF POLICY
# ============================================================



---

# ./governance/130_GOVERNANCE_MODEL.md

GOVERNANCE MODEL

Governance defines how Civilization OS makes, records,
and enforces important decisions.

The governance layer exists to preserve structural integrity,
operational accountability, and long-term system stability.

Core responsibilities

decision authority
approval structure
auditability
policy enforcement
human oversight

Governance must remain compatible with Constitution.

No governance mechanism may bypass:

fail closed
traceability
layer dependency
explicit boundary rules

Governance operates through formal processes rather than
implicit operational habits.

Examples

approval workflow
policy change control
incident escalation
security review

All governance outcomes must be attributable,
reviewable, and reproducible.


---

# ./governance/131_AUDIT_SYSTEM.md

AUDIT SYSTEM

The audit system records critical actions and provides
a verifiable history of what happened, when, and why.

Audit exists to support:

investigation
accountability
regulatory review
post-incident analysis
historical traceability

Audit records should include:

actor identity
operation type
target resource
timestamp
result
related event or request id

Audit data must be immutable once recorded.

Audit records are not merely logs.
They are part of the authoritative evidence layer
of Civilization OS.

Examples of auditable actions

configuration changes
approval decisions
security exceptions
manual overrides
state repair procedures

If an operation cannot be audited,
the operation should not be treated as authoritative.


---

# ./governance/132_APPROVAL_WORKFLOW.md

APPROVAL WORKFLOW

Approval workflow controls actions that require
explicit human or delegated authorization.

Approval is required when an operation may affect:

system structure
security posture
financial exposure
production integrity
cross-domain trust

Typical approval stages

request creation
review
approval or rejection
execution
audit recording

Approval workflow must be explicit.

The system must never assume approval
through silence, omission, or implicit role inference.

Approval decisions must record:

approver
decision
reason
decision timestamp
scope of approval

Approved operations remain subject to Constitution.
Approval cannot legalize structural violations.


---

# ./governance/133_ACCOUNTABILITY_MODEL.md

ACCOUNTABILITY MODEL

Accountability means every critical action can be connected
to a responsible actor and reviewed later.

Actors may include:

human operators
system services
AI agents
scheduled processes

Accountability requires:

identity
authority scope
traceable action history
reviewability

Responsibility must not be hidden inside
anonymous execution flows.

If a system service acts automatically,
the originating policy, trigger, and execution path
must still be attributable.

Accountability protects Civilization OS from:

unowned changes
unreviewable automation
silent state mutation
unclear security actions

Every authoritative action must have
an accountable origin.


---

# ./governance/134_SLA_POLICY.md

SLA POLICY

Service Level Agreements define expected operational quality
for core Civilization services.

SLA exists to make reliability explicit.

Typical SLA dimensions

availability
latency
recovery time
processing success rate
incident response time

SLA must distinguish between:

target
warning threshold
breach threshold

SLA policy must be realistic and measurable.

SLA metrics are used for:

operations review
capacity planning
incident classification
governance escalation

SLA does not override Constitution.

A system must never compromise security or integrity
only to satisfy a superficial availability target.


---

# ./security/140_TRUST_MODEL.md

TRUST MODEL

The trust model defines how Civilization OS decides
whether an action, identity, or event is acceptable.

Trust is not assumed.
Trust is established through verifiable evidence.

Primary trust factors

identity verification
signature verification
key validity
schema compatibility
authorization scope

Trust must be explicit and machine-checkable.

Trust should never depend on:

hidden assumptions
operator memory
silent compatibility
manual guesswork

Trust relationships may exist between:

services
domains
runtime components
operators
AI agents

When trust cannot be established,
the system must fail closed.


---

# ./security/141_KEY_ROTATION_POLICY.md

KEY ROTATION POLICY

Cryptographic keys must be rotated to reduce long-term risk
while preserving verification of historical records.

Key rotation policy defines:

rotation interval
activation process
deactivation process
historical verification strategy
revocation handling

Old keys may stop signing,
but they must remain usable for verifying valid historical events
unless explicitly revoked.

Key rotation must not break:

event verification
auditability
historical replay
snapshot validation

Every rotation must be recorded with:

key id
activation time
retirement time
reason
operator or process


---

# ./security/142_SIGNING_LIFECYCLE.md

SIGNING LIFECYCLE

Signing lifecycle defines how a signing key moves through
its operational states.

Typical lifecycle

generated
registered
activated
used for signing
rotated
retired
revoked if necessary

The lifecycle must be visible and auditable.

Each signing state affects what the system may do:

generated   -> not yet trusted
active      -> may sign
retired     -> may verify history but not sign
revoked     -> must not be trusted

Signing lifecycle must remain compatible with
runtime verification and historical traceability.

No event should become unverifiable
simply because lifecycle handling was neglected.


---

# ./security/143_SECURITY_ESCALATION.md

SECURITY ESCALATION

Security escalation defines how Civilization OS responds
to suspicious or high-risk situations.

Escalation may be triggered by:

signature failure
key mismatch
repeated authorization failure
unexpected state mutation
policy breach
security incident correlation

Escalation levels should be explicit.

Typical stages

detection
alert
containment
review
remediation
closure

Security escalation must produce audit evidence.

Containment may include:

blocking execution
revoking trust
disabling an integration
raising manual review

Escalation must prioritize system integrity
over convenience or speed.


---

# FILE: 080.policy/000_POLICY_OVERVIEW.md

# POLICY OVERVIEW

status: canonical
layer: policy
owner: Boss
prepared_by: Zero
review_required: Sato_DB

## PURPOSE
Provide the unified structure for policy in Civilization OS.

## STRUCTURE
- 010.domain      : domain-specific policy
- 020.governance  : cross-cutting governance policy

## OWNERSHIP
- 070.policy is the only active policy root.




# FILE: 080.policy/010.domain/000.core/000_POLICY_OVERVIEW.md

status: canonical

- DB-controlled values
- event-driven system
- no cross-domain mutation
- no destructive collapse

# ============================================================

layer: policy

- defines behavior, not structure
- all values must be DB-controlled
- no implementation allowed
- no cross-domain ownership

# FILE: 080.policy/020.governance/071_AUTHORITY_POLICY_CANONICAL.md

# ============================================================
# AUTHORITY POLICY
# ============================================================

status: canonical
scope: civilization.policy.authority


# PURPOSE

Define authority hierarchy of Civilization systems.


# AUTHORITY LEVELS

System Authority  
AI Authority  
Human Authority


# AUTHORITY PRINCIPLE

AI cannot override system authority.

Human governance defines final control.


# AUTHORITY CONTROL

Authority must be explicitly defined.

Implicit authority escalation is forbidden.





# FILE: 080.policy/020.governance/071_WAR_POLICY.md

# WAR POLICY

- 宣戦布告必須
- 無制限戦争禁止（Triple制御）




# FILE: 080.policy/020.governance/072_ACCESS_POLICY_CANONICAL.md

# ============================================================
# ACCESS POLICY
# ============================================================

status: canonical
scope: civilization.policy.access


# PURPOSE

Define system access rules.


# ACCESS TYPES

Read Access  
Write Access  
Control Access


# ACCESS PRINCIPLE

Least privilege principle.

Access must be explicitly granted.


# ACCESS CONTROL

Unauthorized access attempts must be blocked.





# FILE: 080.policy/020.governance/072_TIME_POLICY.md

# TIME POLICY

- 時間倍率はサーバー管理
- プレイヤー変更不可




# FILE: 080.policy/020.governance/073_DATA_GOVERNANCE_POLICY_CANONICAL.md

# ============================================================
# DATA GOVERNANCE POLICY
# ============================================================

status: canonical
scope: civilization.policy.data


# PURPOSE

Define data ownership and governance.


# DATA TYPES

System Data  
AI Data  
Civilization Data


# DATA PRINCIPLE

Data integrity must be preserved.

Unauthorized modification is forbidden.


# DATA CONTROL

All data mutations must be logged.





# FILE: 080.policy/020.governance/073_ECONOMY_POLICY.md

# ECONOMY POLICY

- 国家は企業施策に直接介入しない
- 法律で制御する




# FILE: 080.policy/020.governance/074_EVENT_POLICY_CANONICAL.md

# ============================================================
# EVENT POLICY
# ============================================================

status: canonical
scope: civilization.policy.event


# PURPOSE

Define event governance rules.


# EVENT TYPES

System Event  
AI Event  
Civilization Event


# EVENT PRINCIPLE

Events must be immutable.

Event ordering must be preserved.


# EVENT CONTROL

Events cannot be modified after creation.





# FILE: 080.policy/020.governance/075_SECURITY_POLICY_CANONICAL.md

# ============================================================
# SECURITY POLICY
# ============================================================

status: canonical
scope: civilization.policy.security


# PURPOSE

Define system security rules.


# SECURITY OBJECTIVES

Confidentiality  
Integrity  
Availability


# SECURITY PRINCIPLE

Security must be enforced at system boundaries.


# SECURITY CONTROL

Unauthorized system access must be prevented.





# FILE: 080.policy/020.governance/076_AUDIT_POLICY_CANONICAL.md

# ============================================================
# AUDIT POLICY
# ============================================================

status: canonical
scope: civilization.policy.audit


# PURPOSE

Define audit requirements.


# AUDIT TARGETS

System operations  
AI decisions  
Event processing


# AUDIT PRINCIPLE

All critical actions must be traceable.





# FILE: 080.policy/020.governance/077_RUNTIME_CONTROL_POLICY_CANONICAL.md

# ============================================================
# RUNTIME CONTROL POLICY
# ============================================================

status: canonical
scope: civilization.policy.runtime


# PURPOSE

Define runtime control rules.


# CONTROL OBJECTIVE

Prevent unstable runtime behavior.


# CONTROL PRINCIPLE

Runtime operations must follow deterministic rules.





# FILE: 080.policy/020.governance/078_FAILURE_POLICY_CANONICAL.md

# ============================================================
# FAILURE POLICY
# ============================================================

status: canonical
scope: civilization.policy.failure


# PURPOSE

Define failure handling rules.


# FAILURE TYPES

System Failure  
AI Failure  
Data Failure


# FAILURE PRINCIPLE

# FILE: 080.policy/020.governance/079_EVOLUTION_POLICY_CANONICAL.md

# ============================================================
# EVOLUTION POLICY
# ============================================================

status: canonical
scope: civilization.policy.evolution


# PURPOSE

Define system evolution governance.


# EVOLUTION TARGETS

System Architecture  
AI Systems  
Civilization Models


# EVOLUTION PRINCIPLE

Evolution must not break system stability.





# FILE: 080.policy/020.governance/governance/130_GOVERNANCE_MODEL.md

GOVERNANCE MODEL

Governance defines how Civilization OS makes, records,
and enforces important decisions.

The governance layer exists to preserve structural integrity,
operational accountability, and long-term system stability.

Core responsibilities

decision authority
approval structure
auditability
policy enforcement
human oversight

Governance must remain compatible with Constitution.

No governance mechanism may bypass:

fail closed
traceability
layer dependency
explicit boundary rules

Governance operates through formal processes rather than
implicit operational habits.

Examples

approval workflow
policy change control
incident escalation
security review

All governance outcomes must be attributable,
reviewable, and reproducible.




# FILE: 080.policy/020.governance/governance/131_AUDIT_SYSTEM.md

AUDIT SYSTEM

The audit system records critical actions and provides
a verifiable history of what happened, when, and why.

Audit exists to support:

investigation
accountability
regulatory review
post-incident analysis
historical traceability

Audit records should include:

actor identity
operation type
target resource
timestamp
result
related event or request id

Audit data must be immutable once recorded.

Audit records are not merely logs.
They are part of the authoritative evidence layer
of Civilization OS.

Examples of auditable actions

configuration changes
approval decisions
security exceptions
manual overrides
state repair procedures

If an operation cannot be audited,
the operation should not be treated as authoritative.




# FILE: 080.policy/020.governance/governance/132_APPROVAL_WORKFLOW.md

APPROVAL WORKFLOW

Approval workflow controls actions that require
explicit human or delegated authorization.

Approval is required when an operation may affect:

system structure
security posture
financial exposure
production integrity
cross-domain trust

Typical approval stages

request creation
review
approval or rejection
execution
audit recording

Approval workflow must be explicit.

The system must never assume approval
through silence, omission, or implicit role inference.

Approval decisions must record:

approver
decision
reason
decision timestamp
scope of approval

Approved operations remain subject to Constitution.
Approval cannot legalize structural violations.




# FILE: 080.policy/020.governance/governance/133_ACCOUNTABILITY_MODEL.md

ACCOUNTABILITY MODEL

Accountability means every critical action can be connected
to a responsible actor and reviewed later.

Actors may include:

human operators
system services
AI agents
scheduled processes

Accountability requires:

identity
authority scope
traceable action history
reviewability

Responsibility must not be hidden inside
anonymous execution flows.

If a system service acts automatically,
the originating policy, trigger, and execution path
must still be attributable.

Accountability protects Civilization OS from:

unowned changes
unreviewable automation
silent state mutation
unclear security actions

Every authoritative action must have
an accountable origin.




# FILE: 080.policy/020.governance/governance/134_SLA_POLICY.md

SLA POLICY

Service Level Agreements define expected operational quality
for core Civilization services.

SLA exists to make reliability explicit.

Typical SLA dimensions

availability
latency
recovery time
processing success rate
incident response time

SLA must distinguish between:

target
warning threshold
breach threshold

SLA policy must be realistic and measurable.

SLA metrics are used for:

operations review
capacity planning
incident classification
governance escalation

SLA does not override Constitution.

A system must never compromise security or integrity
only to satisfy a superficial availability target.

# FILE: 080.policy/020.governance/security/140_TRUST_MODEL.md

TRUST MODEL

The trust model defines how Civilization OS decides
whether an action, identity, or event is acceptable.

Trust is not assumed.
Trust is established through verifiable evidence.

Primary trust factors

identity verification
signature verification
key validity
schema compatibility
authorization scope

Trust must be explicit and machine-checkable.

Trust should never depend on:

hidden assumptions
operator memory
silent compatibility
manual guesswork

Trust relationships may exist between:

services
domains
runtime components
operators
AI agents

When trust cannot be established,
the system must fail closed.




# FILE: 080.policy/020.governance/security/141_KEY_ROTATION_POLICY.md

KEY ROTATION POLICY

Cryptographic keys must be rotated to reduce long-term risk
while preserving verification of historical records.

Key rotation policy defines:

rotation interval
activation process
deactivation process
historical verification strategy
revocation handling

Old keys may stop signing,
but they must remain usable for verifying valid historical events
unless explicitly revoked.

Key rotation must not break:

event verification
auditability
historical replay
snapshot validation

Every rotation must be recorded with:

key id
activation time
retirement time
reason
operator or process




# FILE: 080.policy/020.governance/security/142_SIGNING_LIFECYCLE.md

SIGNING LIFECYCLE

Signing lifecycle defines how a signing key moves through
its operational states.

Typical lifecycle

generated
registered
activated
used for signing
rotated
retired
revoked if necessary

The lifecycle must be visible and auditable.

Each signing state affects what the system may do:

generated   -> not yet trusted
active      -> may sign
retired     -> may verify history but not sign
revoked     -> must not be trusted

Signing lifecycle must remain compatible with
runtime verification and historical traceability.

No event should become unverifiable
simply because lifecycle handling was neglected.




# FILE: 080.policy/020.governance/security/143_SECURITY_ESCALATION.md

SECURITY ESCALATION

Security escalation defines how Civilization OS responds
to suspicious or high-risk situations.

Escalation may be triggered by:

signature failure
key mismatch
repeated authorization failure
unexpected state mutation
policy breach
security incident correlation

Escalation levels should be explicit.

Typical stages

detection
alert
containment
review
remediation
closure

Security escalation must produce audit evidence.

Containment may include:

blocking execution
revoking trust
disabling an integration
raising manual review

Escalation must prioritize system integrity
over convenience or speed.

# FILE: 080.policy/070_POLICY_OVERVIEW_CANONICAL.md

# ============================================================
# CIVILIZATION POLICY OVERVIEW
# ============================================================

status: canonical
scope: civilization.policy

owner: Boss
prepared_by: Zero


# PURPOSE

Define governance rules of Civilization OS.


# POLICY ROLE

Policy layer defines system control rules.

Architecture defines structure.
Runtime executes behavior.
Policy defines permission and restriction.


# POLICY DOMAINS

Authority Policy  
Access Policy  
Security Policy  
Audit Policy  
Failure Policy  
Evolution Policy


# DESIGN PRINCIPLE

Fail Closed  
Explicit Control  
Traceable Governance  
Deterministic Behavior





# FILE: 080.policy/080_EVENT_DEDUPLICATION_POLICY.md

# ============================================================
# EVENT DEDUPLICATION POLICY
# ============================================================

status: canonical
scope: civilization.policy.event_deduplication
component: event-deduplication-policy
owner: Boss
prepared_by: Zero


# PURPOSE

Ensures that duplicate events do not produce repeated state mutation.


# DUPLICATE SOURCES

Duplicate events may occur due to

network retry  
dispatcher retry  
worker restart  
message re-delivery


# IDENTIFICATION

Events are uniquely identified by

event_id

Optionally verified using

event_hash


# POLICY RULES

If duplicate event detected

reject execution


Duplicate detection may occur at

ingestion layer  
dispatcher layer  
execution layer


# SAFETY PRINCIPLE

Event execution must be idempotent.


# END
# ============================================================

# FILE: 080.policy/081_CIVILIZATION_MACRO_GOVERNOR_POLICY.md

# ============================================================
# CIVILIZATION MACRO GOVERNOR POLICY
# Triple Civilization Balancer
# ============================================================

status: canonical
version: 1.0
compatibility: STRICT
scope: civilization.policy.macro_governor
component: civilization-macro-governor
document_id: CIV-POL-081
owner: Boss
prepared_by: Zero
last_updated: 2026-03-11


# ============================================================
# ABSTRACT
# ============================================================

Defines the Triple Macro Governor system.

The Triple is a macro balancing mechanism
that stabilizes civilization dynamics.

The governor regulates:

Production
Economy
Reproduction

The system never acts as a ruler.

Its role is to maintain long-term stability
of civilization.


# ============================================================
# CORE CONCEPT
# ============================================================

The Triple represents the macro stabilizer
of the CivilizationOS ecosystem.

It monitors global system metrics and applies
indirect corrections when systemic imbalance occurs.


# ============================================================
# THREE AXES OF CONTROL
# ============================================================

Axis 1

Production Control

Axis 2

Economic Control

Axis 3

Reproduction Control


# ============================================================
# PRODUCTION CONTROL
# ============================================================

Targets

• Production volume  
• Resource consumption  
• Labor allocation  
• Inventory saturation  

Objective

Prevent resource collapse and
runaway overproduction.

Control methods

• world-level event throughput control  
• production event priority adjustment  
• growth-axis indirect correction  

Restriction

Direct inventory modification is forbidden.


# ============================================================
# ECONOMIC CONTROL
# ============================================================

Targets

• Credit expansion  
• nation_credit spikes  
• wealth concentration  
• transaction overload  

Objective

Prevent economic bubbles and credit collapse.

Control methods

• nation_credit adjustment coefficient  
• decay intensity adjustment  
• migration cooldown tuning  
• transaction event weight control  

Restriction

Direct currency issuance is forbidden.


# ============================================================
# REPRODUCTION CONTROL
# ============================================================

Targets

• Persona growth rate  
• AI generation frequency  
• Snapshot issuance rate  
• Civilization onboarding speed  

Objective

Prevent uncontrolled population expansion
and identity inflation.

Control methods

• Snapshot issuance limits  
• AI generation throttling  
• growth rate modifiers  

Restriction

Persona deletion is forbidden.


# ============================================================
# DUAL MODE OPERATION
# ============================================================

Mode A

CEO Mode (Normal Operation)

• Focus on business world  
• Monitor KPIs  
• Operate approval hubs  
• Observe economic indicators  

Mode B

Civilization Mode (Emergency)

• Growth regulation  
• Decay amplification  
• Migration throttling  
• World priority adjustment  


# ============================================================
# MODE SWITCH CONDITIONS
# ============================================================

Civilization mode activates when:

average stability < threshold

trust deviation > threshold

nation_credit variance > threshold

event throughput spike detected


# ============================================================
# SYSTEM IMPLEMENTATION
# ============================================================

Core modules

civilization.macro_control

civilization.system_policy

civilization.health_index

civilization.emergency_flag


Edge Function

civilization-macro-governor


Database Tables

civilization.global_metrics

civilization.adjustment_log


# ============================================================
# CIVILIZATION HEALTH INDEX
# ============================================================

Civilization Health Index (CHI)

CHI =

  0.4 * average_stability
+ 0.3 * inverse_trust_variance
+ 0.2 * nation_credit_stability
+ 0.1 * event_load_stability


CHI < 0.4

→ Emergency Mode


# ============================================================
# PHILOSOPHY
# ============================================================

The Triple is not a ruler.

It is a stabilizer.

Its role is to protect civilization
while preserving individual personas.


# ============================================================
# END OF POLICY
# ============================================================

status: canonical
layer: security

Defines cross-cutting security controls.




# FILE: 100.security/010.identity/010_IDENTITY_SECURITY.md

status: canonical
layer: security

Identity boundaries and authentication context.




# FILE: 100.security/010.identity/020_IDENTITY_PROOF_SECURITY.md

# IDENTITY PROOF SECURITY

status: canonical
layer: security
domain: identity

## PURPOSE
Define proof and assurance requirements for actor identity.

## RULES
- identity source must be explicit
- proof level must be classified
- sensitive action may require stronger proof




# FILE: 100.security/020.access/010_ACCESS_SECURITY.md

status: canonical
layer: security

Permissions and access rules govern all protected actions.




# FILE: 100.security/020.access/020_PERMISSION_ESCALATION_SECURITY.md

# PERMISSION ESCALATION SECURITY

status: canonical
layer: security
domain: access

## RULES
- escalation must be explicit
- silent privilege gain is prohibited
- temporary elevation must expire
- elevation must be audited




# FILE: 100.security/030.audit/010_AUDIT_SECURITY.md

status: canonical
layer: security

Critical actions must be logged and reviewable.




# FILE: 100.security/030.audit/020_NONREPUDIATION_SECURITY.md

# NONREPUDIATION SECURITY

status: canonical
layer: security
domain: audit

## RULES
- critical actions must be attributable
- signed or strongly auditable traces are required
- actor, time, target, and result must be recorded




# FILE: 100.security/040.data/010_DATA_SECURITY.md

status: canonical
layer: security

Sensitive data access must be minimized and controlled.




# FILE: 100.security/040.data/020_ENCRYPTION_AND_KEY_SECURITY.md

# ENCRYPTION AND KEY SECURITY

status: canonical
layer: security
domain: data

## RULES
- key ownership must be explicit
- rotation must be defined
- active and retired keys must be distinguishable
- secret material must never be logged




# FILE: 100.security/050.appsec/010_APPLICATION_SECURITY.md

status: canonical
layer: security

Input validation and trusted execution boundaries.




# FILE: 100.security/050.appsec/020_INPUT_VALIDATION_SECURITY.md

# INPUT VALIDATION SECURITY

status: canonical
layer: security
domain: appsec

## RULES
- all external input must be validated
- parsing failure must fail closed
- schema mismatch must be rejected




# FILE: 100.security/050.appsec/030_EVENT_SIGNATURE_SECURITY.md

# EVENT SIGNATURE SECURITY

status: canonical
layer: security
domain: appsec

## RULES
- signature scope must be deterministic
- verification failure must reject execution
- signer identity must be resolvable




# FILE: 100.security/060.ops/010_SECURITY_OPERATIONS.md

status: canonical
layer: security

Security monitoring and incident operations.




# FILE: 100.security/060.ops/020_SECRET_MANAGEMENT_SECURITY.md

# SECRET MANAGEMENT SECURITY

status: canonical
layer: security
domain: operations

## RULES
- secrets must not be embedded in documents
- secret access must be minimized
- rotation and revocation must exist




# FILE: 100.security/060.ops/030_SECURITY_MONITORING_SECURITY.md

# SECURITY MONITORING SECURITY

status: canonical
layer: security
domain: operations

## RULES
- alert sources must be classified
- detection rules must be reviewable
- critical alerts require audit path




# FILE: 100.security/070.threat/010_THREAT_MODEL_SECURITY.md

# THREAT MODEL SECURITY

status: canonical
layer: security
domain: threat

## THREAT AREAS
- identity spoofing
- permission escalation
- event forgery
- state tampering
- replay execution
- audit bypass
- secret leakage

## RESPONSE
Each threat must map to:
- prevention
- detection
- containment
- auditability
